nodes.title: Handle arrays first with a direct length check
nodes.why: Arrays are common here, and `length === 0` is the clearest and fastest emptiness check.
nodes.alternative: Convert arrays to keys and use `Object.keys(obj).length` for everything.
nodes.tradeoff: Simple and efficient for arrays, but it adds a separate branch in the function.

nodes.title: Guard object checks before reading keys
nodes.why: The guard avoids crashes on `null` and keeps object handling explicit and safe.
nodes.alternative: Call `Object.keys(obj)` directly and rely on try/catch.
nodes.tradeoff: More defensive and readable, with a slightly longer condition.

nodes.title: Throw for unsupported input types

anchor.snippet: throw new TypeError('Expected object or array');

nodes.why: Failing fast prevents silent mistakes and tells the caller exactly how to use the function.
nodes.tradeoff: Stricter behavior with clearer errors, but callers must handle exceptions.
